question_text: Many museums charge admission fees while others are free. Do the advantages of charging people to visit a museum outweigh the disadvantages?
introduction: A visit to a museum is undoubtedly one of the most interesting ways to delve into and discover history. Although charging an admission fee to enter a museum is common, it is not always popular. This essay will discuss the advantages and potential drawbacks of charging such a fee and show that it is necessary.

body_paragraphs.text: There are a number of significant benefits to charging an entrance fee. As the main aim of a museum is to promote the enjoyment and sharing of cultural heritage, artefacts must be maintained carefully so they are unaffected by conditions which may impact their value, such as light, dust and accidental damage. Equipment and the hiring of professionals to care for and protect these valuable items would not always be possible without money from the public. Another point is that many museums do not have stable financial support. Those in the UK, for instance, had their funding slashed recently as a result of government policy changes. Clearly, without admission fees, many institutions would not be able to survive.
sentences: There are a number of significant benefits to charging an entrance fee.

As the main aim of a museum is to promote the enjoyment and sharing of cultural heritage, artefacts must be maintained carefully so they are unaffected by conditions which may impact their value, such as light, dust and accidental damage.
Equipment and the hiring of professionals to care for and protect these valuable items would not always be possible without money from the public.
Another point is that many museums do not have stable financial support.
Those in the UK, for instance, had their funding slashed recently as a result of government policy changes.
Clearly, without admission fees, many institutions would not be able to survive.

body_paragraphs.text: Admittedly, however, charging an admission fee may have some negative effects. From the visitors' perspective, this charge may deter them from visiting in the first place. If the fee were $20, families, low-income earners and students may not be able to afford it. In terms of the museum itself, this drop in visitor numbers could affect their ability to employ staff, host exhibitions and in some cases may even result in closure. While such concerns are valid, most museums do keep their prices low or offer discounts to prevent these scenarios.

While such concerns are valid, most museums do keep their prices low or offer discounts to prevent these scenarios.

conclusion: In conclusion, museums play an important role in our communities, which is why it is necessary to consider ways to keep them operating. While admission fees may deter some visitors, the benefits of such a cost, provided it is reasonable, far outweigh this concern.

question_text: Some people believe that it is wrong to keep animals in zoos, while others think that zoos are important for education and conservation. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
introduction: Traditionally places of entertainment, zoos have changed considerably, and mostly for the better. While some feel that they have a crucial role to play both in education and in conservation more broadly, others argue that they are unsuitable places for animals. This essay will discuss both views and outline why I feel zoos are cruel and pointless.

body_paragraphs.text: Advocates of zoos claim that they are an indispensable tool in raising awareness of environmental issues and fighting species extinction. Indeed, many zoos are engaged in captive breeding programs of endangered animals in order to provide a safety net for dwindling wild populations. In Australia, where the Tasmanian Devil population has been decimated by a contagious disease in recent years, Victorian zoos are breeding healthy Devils to bolster the numbers. Such programs, supporters claim, also help to educate the public and lead them towards more sustainable lives. In other words, contact encourages activism, in their view.
sentences: Advocates of zoos claim that they are an indispensable tool in raising awareness of environmental issues and fighting species extinction.
Indeed, many zoos are engaged in captive breeding programs of endangered animals in order to provide a safety net for dwindling wild populations.
In Australia, where the Tasmanian Devil population has been decimated by a contagious disease in recent years, Victorian zoos are breeding healthy Devils to bolster the numbers.
Such programs, supporters claim, also help to educate the public and lead them towards more sustainable lives.
In other words, contact encourages activism, in their view.

body_paragraphs.text: Despite these arguments, others, myself included, feel that keeping animals in captivity is unacceptable. Even though the Tasmanian Devils may thrive in captivity, most animals, particularly large wide-roaming species like lions and elephants, struggle to reproduce. For those that do, their life is spent in small enclosures with limited stimulation and little to no chance of ever returning to the wild. In terms of education, there is no evidence that seeing a captive animal leads to any meaningful shift in behaviour or attitudes. Clearly, zoo supporters' claims are unfounded.

conclusion: In conclusion, this essay discussed reasons why some people support zoos and others oppose them. In my view, while conservation efforts are vital, I feel that there are better ways to go about it than by locking animals up in zoos.

question_text: Thanks to the development of technology, many employers are now offering employees the option to work from home. Is this a positive or negative development?
introduction: With the advancement of technology, the modern workplace has changed dramatically. As a result of the proliferation of communication tools like email, messaging and project management software systems, an increasing number of businesses are now allowing their staff to work from home. In my view, this is a positive step for both companies and workers.

body_paragraphs.text: From the businesses' perspective, there are two key benefits stemming from these flexible working arrangements. The first is a more productive workforce. Indeed, the recent isolation regulations of COVID-19 have proven beyond doubt that people can work even more efficiently and effectively from home. The office, it seems, is actually a distractor to many. The other advantage for a company is that working from home means employees can be recruited globally, not just within commuting distance from work. In other words, if a stellar marketing manager lives on the other side of the world, and is the best fit for the role, there is no reason not to hire them.
sentences: From the businesses' perspective, there are two key benefits stemming from these flexible working arrangements.
The first is a more productive workforce.
Indeed, the recent isolation regulations of COVID-19 have proven beyond doubt that people can work even more efficiently and effectively from home.
The office, it seems, is actually a distractor to many.
The other advantage for a company is that working from home means employees can be recruited globally, not just within commuting distance from work.
In other words, if a stellar marketing manager lives on the other side of the world, and is the best fit for the role, there is no reason not to hire them.

body_paragraphs.text: For the workers themselves the benefits abound. Chief among these is the ability to balance home and work life. Whereas, in the past, a typical working parent had to wake up early, prepare lunches, drive children to school, continue on to work, put in the 8-10 hours in the office before returning home to cook dinner, working from home reduces this load. A further plus is the flexibility the situation affords. Without the need to be present in office hours, workers can complete their tasks at a time that suits them, whether it is midnight or midday. This freedom not only improves the quality of their work, but of their life as well.

Chief among these is the ability to balance home and work life.

This freedom not only improves the quality of their work, but of their life as well.

conclusion: In conclusion, although not every job can be done from home, if it is possible, it can be extremely beneficial. Companies can reap the rewards in terms of productivity and quality staff, while workers are able to manage their time better.

question_text: Stress is now a major problem in many countries around the world. What are some of the factors in modern society that cause this stress, and how can we reduce it?
introduction: Stress seems to have become synonymous with modern lifestyles, and it can have dire consequences for both mental and physical health. It is now a serious concern in many countries. This essay will discuss the two main causes of stress and suggest ways to manage it.

body_paragraphs.text: Certain features of modern society tend to induce stress. One is the stream of information about local and world events bombarding us daily. The internet and, more recently, smartphones have made such events inescapable. These constant notifications about a friend's personal crisis or a natural disaster somewhere in the world can elicit feelings of helplessness and, in turn, anxiety. The modern workplace also exacerbates stress for much the same reason. Whereas the working day used to end at 5:30pm in most cultures, now, employees, regardless of their role, are contactable and often work well beyond normal work hours. The more technology develops, the greater the stress becomes.
sentences: Certain features of modern society tend to induce stress.
One is the stream of information about local and world events bombarding us daily.
The internet and, more recently, smartphones have made such events inescapable.
These constant notifications about a friend's personal crisis or a natural disaster somewhere in the world can elicit feelings of helplessness and, in turn, anxiety.
The modern workplace also exacerbates stress for much the same reason.
Whereas the working day used to end at 5:30pm in most cultures, now, employees, regardless of their role, are contactable and often work well beyond normal work hours.
The more technology develops, the greater the stress becomes.

body_paragraphs.text: Reducing this everyday stress, however, is possible. The first step is to disconnect. We should curb our use of devices after work, turning off notifications, and switching off not only from work, but also from the news cycle and social media. If we do not set boundaries, the invasion of the outside world into our personal and mental space will only grow, inevitably leading to stress and other related illnesses. Another way to address this problem is physical exercise. Both rigorous activity like running or boxing, and even low-impact exercise like yoga and pilates can be beneficial and should be encouraged. They are the fastest way out of stress. The more we take care of our bodies, the healthier our minds will be.

conclusion: Stress is undoubtedly a serious and pervasive problem, compounded by modern technology and work culture. If we draw personal boundaries with work and technology, we should see an overall improvement in our wellbeing.

question_text: Many governments invest a great deal of money in space research. What are the advantages and potential disadvantages of spending money on space exploration?
introduction: Humankind is curious by nature, and it is this curiosity that drives our fascination with outer space. Globally, there are several countries that spend billions annually on space programs that seek to send people to space, discover new planets or simply uncover new information. In this essay, I will outline what I consider to be the two main benefits that can be gained from spending on such research, as well as some drawbacks of this investment.

body_paragraphs.text: Certainly, space exploration can bring about many positive impacts. Perhaps the most significant ones, ironically, are the by-products of the technological developments. Everyday items such as sunglasses, non-stick cookware, and important medical devices like pacemakers all came about thanks to research and development in the space field. Another plus is the benefit to humanity of finding another habitable planet. Indeed, if we continue to deplete our natural resources and pollute our own environment, the money spent on discovering a new planet, or on creating liveable spaces on Mars, for instance, will be well worth it.
sentences: Certainly, space exploration can bring about many positive impacts.
Perhaps the most significant ones, ironically, are the by-products of the technological developments.
Everyday items such as sunglasses, non-stick cookware, and important medical devices like pacemakers all came about thanks to research and development in the space field.
Another plus is the benefit to humanity of finding another habitable planet.

Indeed, if we continue to deplete our natural resources and pollute our own environment, the money spent on discovering a new planet, or on creating liveable spaces on Mars, for instance, will be well worth it.

body_paragraphs.text: Despite these benefits, there are also serious negative outcomes that can result from state spending on space research. The primary one is the diversion of funds from vital public services like healthcare and education. In the USA, for example, despite rocketing numbers of homeless people, and prohibitively expensive hospital care, the government continues to pour money into space programs. There is also the problem of space junk. When rockets and satellites launch and fall into disuse, they become part of the litter that is already cluttering up space. This debris poses potential risks to future missions as well as simply contributing to the global pollution problem.

This debris poses potential risks to future missions as well as simply contributing to the global pollution problem.

conclusion: In conclusion, space research can have life-changing impacts here on Earth. It does, however, come at a potentially huge cost if a nation does not prioritise its spending and neglects the environmental fallout of missions.

question_text: Many people believe that family has the greatest influence on a child’s development, while others argue that other factors such as television, friends and music are more significant. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
introduction: A child’s personality and character are formed in the early years of their life. While some people believe that the primary influence on this development is the child’s family, others feel that external factors, such as media and peers, are more important. In this essay, I will discuss both views and explain why I believe that the family remains the most significant influence.

body_paragraphs.text: On one hand, it is argued that the modern world offers many influences that can shape a child’s growth. Television, for instance, is a pervasive presence in most homes, and children are exposed to a wide range of content from a very young age. This can influence their language, their values, and even their aspirations. Similarly, as children grow older, their peer group becomes increasingly important. They may adopt the styles, interests, and even the behaviors of their friends in order to fit in. Music, too, can play a role, as it often forms a key part of a young person’s identity and can influence their outlook on the world.
sentences: On one hand, it is argued that the modern world offers many influences that can shape a child’s growth.

Similarly, as children grow older, their peer group becomes increasingly important.
They may adopt the styles, interests, and even the behaviors of their friends in order to fit in.
Music, too, can play a role, as it often forms a key part of a young person’s identity and can influence their outlook on the world.

body_paragraphs.text: On the other hand, many people, myself included, believe that the family is the most fundamental influence. This is because the family is the first and most constant presence in a child’s life. It is within the family that a child learns their basic values, their sense of right and wrong, and their primary social skills. The influence of parents and siblings is often subconscious and deeply rooted, forming the foundation upon which all other influences are built. Even when children are exposed to media and peers, they often filter these experiences through the values and beliefs they have learned at home.

conclusion: In conclusion, while it is true that many factors contribute to a child’s development, I believe that the family is the most significant. Although external influences like television and friends are undoubtedly important, they are secondary to the primary influence of the home environment.
